summary: A former CTO asking about returning to engineering should come back as a regular software engineer, not a staff+ role, and spend at least 6 months doing hands-on daily coding with a great team. The transition requires not just refreshing technical skills but re-acclimating to the rhythms of deep focus work, reconnecting with curiosity, and rebuilding empathy for production code — similar to how moving into management required learning an entirely different way of working.
key_points: Return to engineering as a regular software engineer writing code daily, not as a staff+ engineer
Tactical coding skills degrade like language fluency — you use it or lose it — while management skills persist
Start with something new rather than picking up where you left off to avoid painful stumbling through once-effortless motions
Allow at least 6 months to re-acclimate to the deep focus rhythms of engineering work
Reconnecting with the emotional intensity of shipping code and being personally responsible for production is essential
money_quotes: Writing code is like speaking a language: you use it or lose it. And just like with a language, the best way to freshen up is full immersion.

I don't think it's being overly dramatic to say that you have to allow enough time to become a different version of yourself. You can't just change personas and entire ways of being like you change your clothing.

If you try to pick up where you left off as an expert, it's really going to suck as you stumble through the motions that used to feel effortless and automatic. But if you start with something new, the friction of learning will feel ordinary and predictable instead.
Engineers respect people who respect what they do.

To me, 'software architect' rings of 'someone who can no longer write code or perform as a software engineer, who has probably been at the same company for so long that their skills and knowledge now consist entirely of minutiae about that particular company's technology.'
key_insight: The best path back from CTO to engineer is full immersion in daily hands-on coding with a great team, starting fresh with new technologies rather than trying to resume where you left off, because rebuilding tactical skills and reconnecting with the emotional reality of shipping code matters more than preserving your title.
